question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to return a stream that completes normally but contains fewer records than expected, exploiting the fact that clientSubscribe() has no minimum-record-count validation, so the monitor reports success while records are actually missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a wrapped exception (e.g., a RuntimeException wrapping a StatusRuntimeException) to be passed to getStatusCode(), which only checks the top-level type and returns UNKNOWN instead of the actual gRPC status, causing shouldRetry() to retry indefinitely and mask the real reason records are missing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doBeforeRetry log in clientSubscribe() that only logs r.totalRetries() + 1 and a truncated message, so that after many retries the log is flooded with low-information warnings while the actual missing-record condition on the mirror node goes undiagnosed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the for loop in createSubscriptions() creates multiple GrpcSubscription instances sharing the same GrpcSubscriberProperties object, so that a mutation of properties (e.g., topicId) by one subscription affects all others, causing incorrect topic monitoring and missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) in subscribe() to cause a slow or stalled subscription (e.g., one that never emits or errors) to hold up the overall Flux, preventing timely detection of missing records from other mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the retry backoff in clientSubscribe() to grow to maxBackoff (8 seconds) between attempts, creating a window during which missing records on the mirror node are not detected or reported, allowing an export gap to persist unnoticed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to alternate between returning INVALID_ARGUMENT (which stops retries) and other error codes, exploiting the binary shouldRetry() logic to create unpredictable retry behavior that causes the monitor to inconsistently report missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of deduplication in createSubscriptions() to cause multiple GrpcSubscription instances with the same topicId to be created (via properties.getSubscribers() > 1), inflating the apparent record count and masking the fact that some records are missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that clientSubscribe() does not validate the content or completeness of SubscribeResponse items returned by grpcClient.subscribe(), so that a mirror node returning malformed or partial records is counted as successful exports, hiding the true missing-record condition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a rate limit or concurrency cap on the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) in subscribe() to cause resource exhaustion by spawning a large number of concurrent subscriptions (via high subscribers count), degrading the monitor's ability to detect missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cached Flux returned by getSubscriptions() to observe a subscription that was created with a now-expired startTime/endTime window (from GrpcSubscriberProperties.getEndTime()), causing the monitor to report no records received when in fact the subscription window has passed and records are being missed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the doOnError handler in clientSubscribe() to log a subscription object (subscription.toString()) that contains sensitive or misleading information, and since the error only fires after all retries, the log entry appears too late to detect ongoing missing-record conditions on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createSubscriptions() does not validate that the resolved topicId actually exists on the mirror node before creating subscriptions, causing the monitor to silently subscribe to a non-existent topic and report zero records without flagging the missing export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return ABORTED status repeatedly, which passes shouldRetry() and triggers infinite retries, so that a mirror node that is consistently aborting record exports is never detected as a missing-record source?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return OUT_OF_RANGE status (e.g., for a startTime beyond available records), which passes shouldRetry() and triggers infinite retries, causing the monitor to never report that records within the requested range are missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a total-duration timeout on the retry loop in clientSubscribe() to cause the monitor to spend unbounded time retrying a failed subscription, during which the missing-record condition on the mirror node is never escalated or alerted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createSubscriptions() uses Flux.fromIterable(subscriptionList) without any ordering guarantee, causing subscriptions to be processed in an unpredictable order that may result in some topics being monitored later than others, creating windows where missing records go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribe() uses flatMap (unbounded concurrency) rather than flatMap with a concurrency limit, causing all subscriptions to start simultaneously and potentially overwhelming the mirror node, leading to dropped or missing records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the grpcClient.subscribe() to return a Flux that emits items very slowly (e.g., one item per retry interval), exploiting the retry logic in clientSubscribe() to make the monitor believe records are being exported when in fact most records are missing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in getStatusCode() by triggering a StatusRuntimeException with a null status, causing the method to throw instead of returning a code, which would propagate as an unexpected error through shouldRetry() and potentially halt the subscription without proper missing-record reporting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry logic in clientSubscribe() does not distinguish between transient network errors and persistent mirror node export failures, causing the monitor to retry indefinitely on a mirror node that is permanently missing records without ever raising an alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that createSubscriptions() does not check for duplicate topicId values across different GrpcSubscriberProperties entries, causing the monitor to subscribe to the same topic multiple times and double-count records, masking the fact that other topics have missing exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern so that if the first subscriber to the cached Flux triggers createSubscriptions() during a period of misconfiguration, all subsequent subscribers receive the same incorrect subscription list, permanently causing incorrect record export monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux.defer(() -> grpcClient.subscribe(subscription)) in clientSubscribe() to be deferred but the subscription object to be mutated between retries (e.g., topicId changed), causing each retry to subscribe to a different topic and miss records from the originally intended mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a gRPC server to return FAILED_PRECONDITION (e.g., topic not yet created), which passes shouldRetry() and triggers infinite retries, so that the monitor never reports that records for a newly expected topic are missing from the mirror node export?
